highlights.description: Score-to-level mapping with built-in CVSS support, and level counting for dashboards and reports

added.description: `ScoreRange` type for mapping numeric score ranges to framework levels
`CVSSScoreRange()` built-in for CVSS v3 scores (0-10) to Severity levels
`LevelCounts` type for tracking item counts per priority level
`Slice()` returns counts in framework level order

tests.description: Comprehensive tests for ScoreRange and LevelCounts

highlights.description: Go library for standardized priority level definitions with 5 built-in frameworks and cross-framework normalization

added.description: Core `Framework` and `Level` types for defining priority systems
Built-in `Severity` framework (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info)
Built-in `IETF` framework (RFC 2119 requirement levels: MUST, SHOULD, MAY)
Built-in `MoSCoW` framework (Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, Won't Have)
`Normalize()` function for converting levels to comparable numeric scale (0-100)
`CompareAcross()` function for comparing priorities across different frameworks
`MapTo()` function for finding equivalent levels between frameworks

tests.description: Unit tests for framework lookups, normalization, and cross-framework comparison

documentation.description: README with installation, usage examples, and API documentation
Badge shields for CI, Go Report Card, and license
Contributing section with link to CHANGELOG

build.description: GitHub Actions CI workflows for testing, linting, and CodeQL
Dependabot configuration for automated dependency updates
golangci-lint configuration with gosec, staticcheck, and other linters
